title: Livewired
subtitle: The Inside Story of the Ever-Changing Brain
author: David Eagleman
themes: The brain is not pre-wired — it is livewired, constantly adapting to the world and its own inputs
Plasticity is the core feature of the brain, not an add-on feature
The brain dynamically allocates its resources based on input — unused territory is reassigned
Sensory substitution and augmentation show the brain's remarkable ability to adapt to new inputs
Understanding plasticity illuminates everything from development to aging to education
The brain is not a finished product — it is a work in progress
excerpt: Your brain is not a preprogrammed device, not a computer with fixed architecture. It is a dynamic system, constantly rewiring itself based on what it encounters. The brain is not wired — it is livewired, always adjusting, always learning, always adapting to the world it finds itself in.
